latin: Milites Caesaris hesitaverunt ad flumen. 'si transierimus,' centurio dixit, 'hostes rei publicae erimus.' Caesar primus in aquam intravit. ubi dux it, exercitus sequitur.

questions.target: They would become enemies of the Republic
choices: They would become enemies of the Republic

translation: Caesar's soldiers hesitated at the river. 'If we cross,' a centurion said, 'we will be enemies of the Republic.' Caesar was the first to enter the water. Where the leader goes, the army follows.
